steps.title: Identify the additive relationship
steps.content:
The passage states that the photograph fuels exploration of love and cultural identity, then moves to what else the poem does: reflect on relationships between poetry and other art forms.

The key signal is that these are two complementary observations about the poem's response, requiring an additive transition.

steps.title: Match the transition to the relationship
steps.content:
The logical relationship is addition — the poem both explores love and cultural identity AND reflects on relationships between poetry and other art forms. The blank needs a transition signaling a second, reinforcing point.
  • Choice A — "Conversely,": signals a direct contrast or reversal, so fails — reflecting on art forms is complementary to exploring identity, not opposite to it. ✗
  • Choice B — "What's more,": signals an additional point that builds on what preceded it, so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — "For instance,": signals a specific example of a general claim, so fails — the second observation is a new point, not an illustration of the first. ✗
  • Choice D — "In other words,": signals restatement of the same idea, so fails — the two observations are distinct, not paraphrases of each other. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
